problem.statement: Platform operators need one governed progressive-delivery lifecycle that releases only an independently approved feature-flag change to an explicit audience, region or surface, blocks unhealthy progression, separates pause from resume, supports abort and rollback, and leaves an immutable recovery trail.
problem.currentWorkaround: The platform-control service already stores rollouts and can advance, pause, abort and restore a flag baseline, but resume is implicit inside advance, target scope accepts ungoverned JSON, health-gate failures are not recorded as operational alerts, and no canonical recovery guide is exposed for support.

actors.role: Authorized maker of a platform change set
permittedActions: propose and validate a change set
forbiddenActions: create a rollout from an unapplied change

actors.role: Independent checker for the underlying platform change
permittedActions: approve or reject a submitted change
forbiddenActions: operate a rollout created from the same change when maker-checker separation would be lost

actors.role: Authorized control-panel operator running the approved rollout lifecycle
permittedActions: create from an applied feature-flag change
forbiddenActions: target an empty or unknown scope

actors.role: Authorized reader using audit and recovery guidance during incidents
forbiddenActions: perform rollout mutations without manage permission

actors.role: Sole owner of rollout state, feature-flag revision checks, health gating, audit and baseline restoration
forbiddenActions: write DSH or WLT business truth

actors.role: Consumer surface evaluating the canonical feature flag and targeting projection
permittedActions: apply the platform-owned percentage and scope
forbiddenActions: persist mock targeting as runtime truth

surfaces.exclusionReason: The journey exposes no client-operated rollout UI; the client only consumes the controlled effect.
The partner app does not own rollout operations.
The captain app does not own rollout operations.
The field app does not own rollout operations.

outcome.statement: Every progressive release begins from an applied independently approved feature-flag change, targets an explicit governed scope, advances only from running state after the configured health gate passes, separates pause from resume, restores the captured baseline on abort or rollback, and exposes immutable audit plus deterministic recovery guidance.
outcome.primaryMetric: healthy governed rollout transitions with canonical readback
guardrailMetrics: advance after failed health gate count
outcome.baselineState: Progressive rollout storage and basic transitions exist, but resume, target-scope governance, blocked-health audit and recovery guidance are incomplete.
outcome.targetState: Platform Control owns a complete explicit and auditable progressive-delivery lifecycle with safe recovery.
outcome.observationWindow: targeted product contract static database backend frontend runtime security governance CI and independent review

criteria: A rollout references an applied change set containing the same feature flag and preserves maker-checker separation.
Target scope contains at least one supported nonempty audience, region, city, surface or surfaces selector.
Steps are strictly increasing, bounded by 1..100 and finish at 100.
Advance is legal only from running and cannot implicitly resume a paused rollout.
Resume changes only paused to running and does not change percentage, step or feature-flag revision.
Every advance evaluates required aggregate and service health before changing the flag.
A blocked health gate creates an immutable correlated audit event.
Abort is legal from running or paused and restores the captured baseline with revision checks.
Rollback is legal only from completed and restores the captured baseline with revision checks.
Recovery guidance identifies the legal next action and references the approved change rollback plan.
Control-panel actions are permission gated and refresh canonical server state after mutation.
No target surface or frontend stores a local rollout truth or mock runtime result.

business: Platform Control exclusively owns rollout state and feature-flag mutation.
Every rollout derives from an applied independently approved change set.
Pause and resume are distinct transitions.
Health is evaluated immediately before every advance.
Abort and rollback restore the captured baseline under revision control.
Every mutation and blocked health gate is correlated and audited.
negative: No rollout is created for an unapplied or unrelated change.
No empty or arbitrary target JSON is accepted.
No unhealthy advance changes the feature flag.
No stale revision overwrites a newer flag.
No frontend or target surface owns rollout truth.
No implementation actor self-approves protected product release or production closure.

execution.appetite: Close JRN-041 code data contract and control-panel gaps without deploying production or self-approving protected release evidence.
fixedConstraints: Repository mode is REMOTE_ONLY on sambassam.
Platform Control remains the sole rollout owner.
No PR merge force push release production deployment or SaaS activation is authorized.
Independent product security QA release and risk approvals cannot be self-issued by engineering.
